name: Bigeye Data Observability API
what_it_does: ML-powered data observability platform with REST API — automatically monitoring data pipelines for freshness, volume, distribution, and schema changes without writing manual checks, using machine learning to detect anomalies and surface data quality issues.
use_cases: Automatically monitoring data warehouse tables for anomalies without manual check writing
Querying data quality alert history and anomaly data via REST API
Integrating Bigeye alerts into incident management or Slack for data team notification
Monitoring data freshness and SLA compliance for critical business data assets
Querying data quality scores across tables for executive data reliability reporting
not_for: Manual SQL-based data quality checks (use Soda or Great Expectations)
best_when: A data team wants automatic, ML-powered data quality monitoring without writing individual checks, especially for large numbers of tables and business-critical pipelines.
avoid_when: You prefer explicit, developer-defined checks over ML-inferred anomaly thresholds, or need catalog/lineage features.

auth.notes: API token from Bigeye settings. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Webhook for alert notifications.

pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing. US-based (San Francisco). Founded 2019. Backed by GV, Sequoia, Andreessen. ML-powered automatic monitoring differentiates from manual check tools. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift support. Growing data observability market.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for assets, metrics, alerts, and reports. Bearer token auth. Webhook for alert events. Good documentation for core operations. ML-powered monitoring means thresholds are inferred — query API to understand current thresholds. Enterprise pricing limits accessibility.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert reads fully idempotent. Asset management idempotent by identifier. Metric reads idempotent. Alert acknowledgment idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: ML thresholds are inferred — agent must query current thresholds to understand expected ranges
Enterprise pricing — API access requires sales engagement
Data warehouse connection credentials required before monitoring begins
Alert fatigue possible during initial ML training period — tune sensitivity

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. US data residency. Bearer token auth. Warehouse credentials stored securely. Read-only warehouse access for monitoring. Data quality metadata stored, not raw data. Standard enterprise SaaS security.
